pages.title: Frameworks | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents operations for managing green frameworks in ESG Credits, including creating frameworks, adding year-specific details, registering category indicators with ESG impact values, and retrieving framework data. Supports both private and public framework visibility for green bonds and sustainability projects.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new green framework with identifier, name, currency, and visibility settings
Retrieves all green frameworks for a specific reporting year with optional visibility filter

useCases: Create green bond frameworks with environmental impact categories
Register renewable energy and energy efficiency project impacts
Track allocated amounts and value outstanding for sustainability frameworks
Add category-specific indicators like annual emission reduction or energy production
Retrieve framework details for impact reporting and verification

implementationSteps.title: Create framework
implementationSteps.description: Use addFramework operation to create a new green framework with identifier, name, currency code, and visibility settings

implementationSteps.title: Add year-specific data
implementationSteps.description: Call addYearSpecificsToFramework to register value outstanding, allocated amount, and reporting year for the framework

implementationSteps.title: Register impact indicators
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryIndicatorToFramework to add sub-categories, volumes, indicators, and ESG benefit values for each impact area

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve framework data
implementationSteps.description: Call getFrameworks or getFrameworkDetails to access complete framework information including all categories, indicators, SDGs, and EU objectives

pages.title: Impacts | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides operations for calculating and retrieving ESG impacts from green asset investments. Supports position-level, investor-level, and portfolio-level impact calculations with optional filtering by categories, indicators, and language. Can generate JSON data or PDF reports from templates.

codeExamples.description: Calculates and stores the ESG impact of a specific investment position based on amount, currency, term, and reporting year

useCases: Calculate carbon emission reduction from renewable energy investments
Generate investor impact reports showing environmental benefits
Consolidate portfolio-level ESG impacts across multiple green assets
Calculate impacts without storing investor data for privacy
Track impacts by categories like renewable energy, energy efficiency, and circular economy

implementationSteps.title: Register position
implementationSteps.description: Create an investment position with investor, asset, amount, currency, and term details

implementationSteps.title: Calculate impacts
implementationSteps.description: Use calculatePositionImpacts to compute ESG benefits based on the framework allocation methodology, or use calculatePositionImpactsOnly for calculations without data storage

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve impact data
implementationSteps.description: Call getPositionImpacts, getInvestorImpacts, or getPortfolioImpacts to retrieve calculated environmental benefits with optional filters and report generation

Any impact data is removed when the associated position is removed. getPositionImpacts Returns the impacts of an investment in a green asset POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular position. The operation can return either a JSON formatted data set, or PDF file based on your template which you can provide to your customer as an investor report. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are stacked on each other. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getInvestorImpacts parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor asset_identifier string no For a specified asset of an investor reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy decryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information category_only number/integer no Default is all sub_category_only number/integer no Default is all super_indicator_only number/integer no Default is all indicator_only number/integer no Default is all decimals number/integer no Default is 0 decimals language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Example code curl getPortfolioImpacts Returns the impacts of an investor's portfolio of investments in green assets POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular portfolio. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are consolidated when possibel (when the green framworks are the same). Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getPortfolioImpacts parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description portfolio_identfier string yes String identifier for a portfolio asset_identifier string no For a specified asset in a portfolio reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy decryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information category_only number/integer no Default is all sub_category_only number/integer no Default is all super_indicator_only number/integer no Default is all indicator_only number/integer no Default is all decimals number/integer no Default is 0 decimals language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. Example code curl calculatePositionImpacts Calculates and stores the impact of an investor's investment in a green asset POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a particular position already registered in ESG.Credits. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string calculatePositionImpacts parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description position_id number/integer yes Integer identifier for a position position_amount number yes Amount of money invested position_currency_code string yes Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies position_days number/integer no The term of the investment, default is 365 days reporting_year number/ 4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy Example code curl calculatePositionImpactsOnly Calculates and returns the impact of an investment in a green asset, without having to specify the investor and without storing the result POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a position without any data is stored or logged in ESG.Credits. This operation is applicable for cases when financial institutes wants to utilise ESG.Credits only for calculations and store and investor data in own data repositories. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string calculatePositionImpactsOnly parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description asset_identifier string yes String identifier for an asset position_amount number yes Amount of money invested position_currency_code string yes Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies position_days number/integer no The term of the investment, default is 365 days reporting_year number/4 digits yes Year of the taxonomy category_only number/integer no Default is all sub_category_only number/integer no Default is all super_indicator_only number/integer no Default is all indicator_only number/integer no Default is all decimals number/integer no Default is 0 decimals language_code string no Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language. paragraphs: This section deals with the impact from the green assets, as per the particular investments made in them. Any impact data is removed when the associated position is removed.

Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular position. The operation can return either a JSON formatted data set, or PDF file based on your template which you can provide to your customer as an investor report.
check Operation parameters
integer identifier for a investment
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Input report template name
Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular investor. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are stacked on each other.
check Operation parameters
String identifier for an investor
For a specified asset of an investor
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Use this operation to retrieve the ESG impacts of a particular portfolio. Here the impacts of multiple investments/positions are consolidated when possibel (when the green framworks are the same).
check Operation parameters
String identifier for a portfolio
For a specified asset in a portfolio
For decrypting personally identifiable information
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.
Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a particular position already registered in ESG.Credits.
calculatePositionImpacts
check Operation parameters
Integer identifier for a position
Amount of money invested
position_currency_code
Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies
The term of the investment, default is 365 days

Use this operation to calculate the ESG impacts for a position without any data is stored or logged in ESG.Credits. This operation is applicable for cases when financial institutes wants to utilise ESG.Credits only for calculations and store and investor data in own data repositories.
calculatePositionImpactsOnly
check Operation parameters
String identifier for an asset
Amount of money invested
position_currency_code
Currency code of the investment, as per getCurrencies
The term of the investment, default is 365 days
Default is 0 decimals
Language codes as per getLanguages. Default is the original language.

pages.title: Investors | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents operations for managing investors in ESG Credits solution, including creating investors, generating random identifiers, encrypting personally identifiable information (PII), and retrieving investor lists. Supports encryption keys to protect sensitive investor data while maintaining ESG impact tracking capabilities.

codeExamples.description: Creates or updates an investor record with encrypted personally identifiable information
Generates a unique encryption key for encrypting and decrypting investor PII data

useCases: Register investors for green bond and ESG investment tracking
Generate random investor identifiers to avoid using PII
Encrypt sensitive investor information for GDPR compliance
Retrieve investor lists with decrypted data for reporting
Manage investor records with multi-currency support

implementationSteps.title: Generate encryption key
implementationSteps.description: Call generateEncryptionKey to create a secure key for encrypting personally identifiable information, and store it safely

implementationSteps.title: Create investor identifier
implementationSteps.description: Use generateInvestorIdentifier to create a random identifier, or use your own identifier system

implementationSteps.title: Add investor
implementationSteps.description: Call addInvestor with investor details, currency code, email, and the encryption key to securely store investor information

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve investors
implementationSteps.description: Use getInvestors with the decryption key to view investor lists with decrypted PII when needed

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits Solution Overview ESG Credits API Taxonomy Translations Currencies Frameworks Projects Issuers Assets Investors Positions Portfolios Impacts Blockchain Users X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions ESG Credits ESG Credits API Investors This section has operations to manage investors, i.e. showing, adding, encrypting, and removing investors getInvestors Returns all investors POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all investors. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getInvestors parameters JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Copy [ { "investor_id" : 19 , "investor_identifier" : "3a6515809856" , "investor_name" : "John Smith" , "investor_currency_code" : "USD" , "investor_email" : " [email protected] " } , { "investor_id" : 20 , "investor_identifier" : "9c809eb86ee6" , "investor_name" : "Mary Smith" , "investor_currency_code" : "USD" , "investor_email" : " [email protected] " } ] Copy { "message" : "No investors are registered." } Copy { "message" : "Not authorized." } Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description depryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information user_identifier string no For limiting the output to the investors that the user has access to Example code curl generateInvestorIdentifier Generates a random investor identifier POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to generate investor identifiers, for cases where they need to random and not personally identifiable information (PII). Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string generateInvestorIdentifier Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Example code curl generateEncryptionKey Generates an encryption key used to encrypt personal identifiable information POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to generate an encryption, used to encrypt and decrypt personally identiable infomation (PII) in the data repository of ESG.Credits. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string generateEncryptionKey Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Example code curl addInvestor Creates and updates the information of an existing investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create investors stored in ESG.Credits. Is also used to alter already existing investor data. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addInvestor parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description investor_identifier string yes String identifier for an investor, potentially personally identifiable information (subject to encryption) investor_name string yes Personally identiable information (subject to encryption) investor_currency_code string yes Currency code of the investor, as per getCurrencies (currently not utilized) investor_email string no For investor correspondence e.g investor reports encryption_key string no For encrypting personally identifiable information Example code curl removeInvestor Removes an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove investors from the ESG.Credits data repository. pages.title: Issuers | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides operations for managing issuers of green assets in ESG Credits, including adding, retrieving, and removing issuer information. Supports public and private visibility settings to control cross-account issuer data sharing for green bonds and sustainability-linked assets.

codeExamples.description: Creates or updates an issuer of green assets with identifier, name, currency, and visibility settings
Retrieves all issuers with optional filtering by public or private visibility

useCases: Register banks and financial institutions issuing green bonds
Track corporate issuers of sustainability-linked assets
Manage issuer data with public visibility for shared marketplaces
Maintain private issuer records for internal portfolio tracking
Associate green assets with their issuing organizations

implementationSteps.title: Add issuer
implementationSteps.description: Use addIssuer operation to create an issuer record with unique identifier, name, base currency code, and visibility setting (public or private)

implementationSteps.title: Link to assets
implementationSteps.description: When creating green assets, reference the issuer_identifier to associate assets with their issuing organization

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve and manage
implementationSteps.description: Call getIssuers to list registered issuers, and use removeIssuer to delete issuers without associated assets

Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getIssuers parameters JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error messagee Copy [ { "issuer_id" : 1 , "issuer_identifier" : "35f0c1a1c08" , "issuer_name" : "X Bank" , "issuer_currency_code" : "EUR" , "issuer_visibility" : "public" } , { "issuer_id" : 6 , "issuer_identifier" : "good-bank-issuer" , "issuer_name" : "Good Bank" , "issuer_currency_code" : "SEK" , "issuer_visibility" : "private" } ] Copy { "message" : "No such issuer is registered." } Copy { "message" : "Not authorized." } Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl addIssuer Creates or updates information about an existing issuer POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to add issuers of green assets. This operation also alters already existing issuer information. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string addIssuer parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_identifier string yes String identifier for an issuer issuer_name string yes Issuer name issuer_currency_code string yes Currency code of the asset, as per getCurrencies issuer_visibility string no Public or private. Default is private, meaning not visible across different BWS accounts. Example code curl removeIssuer Removes an issuer POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to remove issuers of green assets from the ESG.Credits data repository. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string removeIssuer parameters * JSON check Operation parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description issuer_identifier string yes String identifier for an issuer Example code curl Previous Projects Next Assets Last updated 20 days ago Was this helpful? getIssuers Returns private or public issuers addIssuer Creates or updates information about an existing issuer removeIssuer Removes an issuer Was this helpful? Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "getIssuers", "parameters": { "issuer_visibility": "public" } } Copy { "message": "Issuer was successfully added." } Copy { "message": "Invalid issuer visibility term used." } Copy { "message": "Not authorized." } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "addIssuer", "parameters": { "issuer_identifier": "good_issuer", "issuer_name": "Good Bank", "issuer_currency_code": "EUR", "issuer_visibility": "private" } } Copy { "Issuer was successfully removed." } Copy { "message": "Issuer was not removed. Asset(s) exist." } Copy { "message": "Not authorized." } Copy curl --location 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' \ --header 'X-Api-Key: API-KEY' \ --header 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "solution": "BWS.ESG.Credits", "operation": "removeIssuer", "parameters": { "issuer_identifier": "good_issuer" }

pages.title: Portfolios | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents operations for managing portfolios of green assets, including creating portfolios, adding/removing positions, and retrieving portfolio compositions. Enables investors to group green investments for consolidated impact reporting and portfolio-level ESG benefit tracking.

codeExamples.description: Creates or updates a portfolio for an investor with a unique portfolio identifier
Adds an investment position to a specific portfolio for consolidated impact tracking

useCases: Create themed portfolios like renewable energy or sustainable infrastructure
Group green bond investments by portfolio for consolidated reporting
Track portfolio-level ESG impacts across multiple assets
Generate portfolio impact reports for investor communications
Manage multiple portfolios per investor with different investment strategies

implementationSteps.title: Create portfolio
implementationSteps.description: Use addPortfolio operation to create a portfolio for an investor with a unique identifier

implementationSteps.title: Add positions
implementationSteps.description: Call addPositionToPortfolio to associate specific investment positions with the portfolio using position_id

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve portfolio data
implementationSteps.description: Use getPortfolios to view all portfolios and assets, or getPortfolioPositions to see detailed position information within a portfolio

implementationSteps.title: Calculate consolidated impacts
implementationSteps.description: Call getPortfolioImpacts to retrieve aggregated ESG benefits across all positions in the portfolio

Default is all. user_identifier string no For limiting the output to the investors that the user has access to decryption_key string no For decrypting personally identifiable information Example code curl getPortfolioPositions Returns all investments in green assets in a portfolio of an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to retrieve all green investments in an investors portfolio. Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.ESG.Credits operation * string getPortfolioPositions parameters * JSON check Operation Parameters Example responses 200: OK Successful operation (example) 400: Bad Request Error message (example) 401: Unauthorized Error message Operation Parameters Parameter Type/Format Required Description portfolio_identifier string yes String identifier for a portfolio Example code curl addPortfolio Creates and updates the information of an existing portfolio of an investor POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Use this operation to create a portfolio for an investor, next to be associated with specific investments. Impacts can then be consolidated on portfolio level. pages.title: Positions | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Provides operations for managing investment positions in green assets, including creating, retrieving, and removing positions. Tracks investor investments with amount, currency, term, and start date, enabling ESG impact calculations based on specific investment allocations in green bonds and sustainability-linked assets.

codeExamples.description: Creates or updates an investment position for an investor in a green asset with amount, currency, term, and start date
Retrieves all investment positions for an investor with optional filtering by asset and issuer

useCases: Register investor purchases of green bonds with investment amounts
Track investment positions across multiple green assets and issuers
Store position terms and start dates for impact calculation periods
Update existing positions when investment amounts change
Remove positions and associated impact data when investments are sold

implementationSteps.title: Create position
implementationSteps.description: Use addPosition operation to register an investment with investor identifier, asset identifier, amount, currency code, term (days), and optional start date

implementationSteps.title: Calculate impacts
implementationSteps.description: If reporting_year is included, impact calculation is triggered automatically; otherwise call calculatePositionImpacts separately

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve positions
implementationSteps.description: Call getPositions with investor identifier to view all investments, with optional filters by asset or issuer, and decryption key for PII

implementationSteps.title: Remove positions
implementationSteps.description: Use removePosition to delete an investment and its associated impact records when no longer needed

pages.title: Projects | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents operations for managing green projects in ESG Credits, including creating projects, adding year-specific details, registering impact indicators, and building frameworks from consolidated project data. Supports detailed tracking of renewable energy, energy efficiency, and circular economy projects with environmental benefit metrics.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new green project with identifier, name, currency, and associated framework for impact tracking
Consolidates impact data from multiple projects into a single green framework for reporting

useCases: Track individual renewable energy projects like wind parks and solar farms
Register project-level environmental impacts and allocated funding
Build green bond frameworks by consolidating multiple project impacts
Monitor energy efficiency and circular economy project benefits
Manage project visibility for public or private reporting

implementationSteps.title: Create project
implementationSteps.description: Use addProject to register a green project with identifier, name, currency, associated framework, and visibility setting

implementationSteps.title: Add year-specific details
implementationSteps.description: Call addYearSpecificsToProject to register allocated amount and reporting year for the project

implementationSteps.title: Register impact indicators
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategoryIndicatorToProject to add sub-categories, volumes, indicators, and ESG benefit values for each impact area

implementationSteps.title: Consolidate into framework
implementationSteps.description: Call buildFrameworkFromProjects to aggregate project impacts into a green framework for portfolio-level reporting

pages.title: Taxonomy | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Documents taxonomy management operations including UN Sustainable Development Goals, EU Environmental Objectives, and custom taxonomy elements like categories, sub-categories, indicators, and units. Enables building structured ESG impact taxonomies for green asset classification and environmental benefit measurement.

codeExamples.description: Adds a new category to the ESG taxonomy for a specific reporting year with language code
Associates UN SDGs and EU Environmental Objectives with a sub-category for impact alignment

useCases: Build custom ESG taxonomies with categories like renewable energy and energy efficiency
Map green assets to UN Sustainable Development Goals
Align investments with EU Environmental Objectives
Create indicators for measuring carbon reduction and energy production
Define measurement units for environmental impact reporting

implementationSteps.title: Define categories and sub-categories
implementationSteps.description: Use addCategory and addSubCategory to create the top-level structure of your ESG taxonomy for each reporting year

implementationSteps.title: Add indicators and units
implementationSteps.description: Call addUnit to define measurement units, then create super indicators and indicators for measuring specific environmental benefits

implementationSteps.title: Link SDGs and EU Objectives
implementationSteps.description: Use addGoalObjectiveToSubCategory to associate sub-categories with relevant UN SDGs and EU Environmental Objectives

implementationSteps.title: Retrieve taxonomy elements
implementationSteps.description: Call getCategories, getSubCategories, getIndicators, getUnits, getSDGs, and getEUObjectives to view the complete taxonomy structure

codeExamples.description: Retrieves all registered languages available for taxonomy translations
Adds or updates a Swedish language translation for a category in the 2023 taxonomy
Adds a Swedish translation for an environmental indicator in the 2022 reporting year

useCases: Create multilingual ESG reporting frameworks for international investors
Translate environmental impact categories into local languages for regulatory compliance
Provide localized taxonomy descriptions for regional stakeholders
Support multi-country ESG disclosure requirements with language-specific reporting
Enable language switching in investor portals for ESG impact data

pages.title: Users | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API documentation for managing end users, groups, and investor access control in ESG Credits. Provides operations to create users, assign them to groups (bankers, investors, regulators), and control which investors' data each user can access with optional encryption support.

codeExamples.description: Creates or updates a user with encrypted personal information in ESG Credits
Assigns a user to the 'bankers' group to control access to specific functionality
Grants a user access to view specific investor's ESG portfolio and impact data

useCases: Control which bankers can view specific investor portfolios and ESG impact reports
Assign regulatory access to compliance officers for ESG audit purposes
Create role-based permissions for financial advisors managing client green investments
Enable investor self-service portals with restricted access to their own ESG data
Manage multi-tenant access for certification bodies reviewing green project frameworks

implementationSteps.title: Create User Accounts
implementationSteps.description: Use addUser operation with user_identifier, name, email, and role to create or update user profiles with optional encryption

implementationSteps.title: Assign Users to Groups
implementationSteps.description: Call addGroupToUser with group names (bankers, investors, regulators, etc.) to define user access levels

implementationSteps.title: Grant Investor Access
implementationSteps.description: Use addInvestorToUser to connect users with specific investors whose ESG data they are authorized to view

pages.title: Solution Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of BWS ESG Credits solution for financial institutions to add ESG impact reporting to digital channels. Supports ICMA framework calculations, multilingual taxonomies, multiple currencies, UN SDG classifications, and blockchain-based impact certification for green asset investments.
useCases: Banks providing ESG impact reports to retail customers investing in green bonds
Investment advisors creating consolidated impact reports across client portfolios
Venture capitalists reporting project-level environmental benefits to sponsors
Issuers publishing impact characteristics of green assets for promotional purposes
Certification bodies digitally publishing quality-assured impact data for green projects

implementationSteps.title: Define Taxonomy and Frameworks
implementationSteps.description: Build your ESG taxonomy with categories, indicators, and units using ICMA or custom frameworks, supporting UN SDGs and EU Environmental Objectives

implementationSteps.title: Register Green Assets and Projects
implementationSteps.description: Input green project benefit characteristics and create framework impact profiles that form the basis for calculations

implementationSteps.title: Generate Impact Reports
implementationSteps.description: Calculate and display investor-specific ESG impact reports based on their investment mix, with optional blockchain certification for immutable proof

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube ESG Credits Solution Overview ESG Credits API X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions ESG Credits Solution Overview BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include: Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market. Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets. Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix. Issuers of green assets that want to provide banks, advisors, and investors with impact details for promotional purposes, and for use in impact calculations. Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets. By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting. Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit. Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework. You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable. The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community. Previous ESG Credits Next ESG Credits API Last updated 20 days ago Was this helpful? Was this helpful?

paragraphs: BWS.ESG.Credits solution overview

This API solution is designed for financial institutions that want to quickly add ESG impact benefits to their digital channels and investment reporting. The benefits come from investments made in green assets. Typical use cases include:
Banks and investment advisors who want to create and provide investor reports to their broad customer base in digital channels, for the specific scope of green assets they market.
Institutional investors in green assets who want to create and view investment reports for their own investment portfolio of broadly available green assets.
Venture capitalists funding green projects that want to create and provide their sponsors with investor reports on both project and consolidated levels, for each sponsor’s investment mix.
Certification bodies and agents that want to digitally publish quality-assured impact characteristics of green projects and green frameworks to issuers of green assets.

By utilizing this API solution, your development team can accelerate the development of your ESG impact reporting solution. The underlying information model and calculation methods are designed to make it easy for you to add impact reporting to your digital channels and investment reporting.

Impact reporting can be done as per the ICMA framework or any taxonomy of choice. The solution supports two levels of impact categories and two levels of impact indicators, as well as any impact unit.

Impact amounts are calculated per annum or as per the term of the financial instrument. The green frameworks' “impact profile” forms the basis for calculations. Benefit characteristics of the green projects invested in can be consolidated and form your green framework.

You can tie United Nations SDGs and EU’s Environmental Objectives classifications to taxonomy impact categories as per your choice. Any combination of currencies are supported, and your choice of taxonomy language can be accessed interchangeable.

The ESG impact profile of your framework can be immutably stored in a blockchain to demonstrate your committment. Blockchain certificates for your customers' specific investment in green assets can also be issued with help of ESG.Credits. Finally, the blockchain can be utilized to ensure that green project benefits are not double-counted across the debt capital community.

implementationSteps.title: Create New Calendar

implementationSteps.title: Handle Pagination
implementationSteps.description: Include lastEvaluatedKey from response in subsequent list_cubes calls to retrieve additional cubes from large fields

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Cubes Represents a zone or tile within a Field . Each Cube is a unique NFT owned by a fan. It tracks ownership, enables rewards, and reflects live event impacts during matches. Cubes are generated automatically when creating a new field. list_cubes POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_cubes parameters * JSON check method parameters new_field Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption fieldId string Field Id. limit number The maximum number of cubes returned (a field can have hundreds of cubes). lastEvaluatedKey (optional) If more cubes are available, include the returned key to get the next chink of cubes. list_cubes API Call Example list_cubes API call using Node.js Copy const axios = require ( 'axios' ); /* build request to use BWS Badges solution */ const request = { { "solution" : "BWS.NFT.GameCube" , "operation" : "list_cubes" , "parameters" : { "fieldId" : "5a1486f3-753c-4194-b2d4-ea68b6e420c2" , "limit" : 5 } } /* call BWS API using Axios */ let config = { method : 'post' , maxBodyLength : Infinity , url : 'https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call' , headers : { 'X-Api-Key' : 'XqaLg...... A5k2V729v' , /* use your API key here! */ 'Content-Type' : 'application/json' } , data : JSON .stringify (request) }; axios.request(config) .then((response) => { console .log ( JSON .stringify ( response .data)); }) .catch((error) => { console .log (error); }); list_cubes Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns a list of cubes and their related cube data, including the cubeId . If more cubes are available, the field lastEvaluatedKey is returned (include it on the API call for getting the next chunk of cubes). update_cube-price POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_cube-price parameters JSON check method parameters update_cube-price Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption field_id string Field Id cubesList list A list of cubes whose price needs to be updated. Use cube data i and j indexes to identify each cube using the format i_j (e.g. 40_0). priceInCents number Price in USD cents (e.g. 500 to indicate a 5USD cube price) update_cube-price API Call Example new_field API call using Node.js update_cube-price Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the number of correctly updated cubes, the number of invalid detected cubes, and the list of invalid cubes. Configure field dimensions for different sports (football, cricket, chess)
Generate hundreds of NFT cubes automatically based on field size and cube dimensions
Link physical sports venues to digital fan engagement platforms

implementationSteps.title: Define Field Parameters
implementationSteps.description: Specify field name, sport type, dimensions in centimeters (fieldLength, fieldWidth), and cube size (cubeLength) for tokenization

implementationSteps.title: Create Field and Cubes
implementationSteps.description: Call new_field operation which automatically generates all cubes for the field based on specified dimensions and returns a fieldId

implementationSteps.title: Manage Field Lifecycle
implementationSteps.description: Use list_fields to view created fields and delete_field to remove fields and their associated cubes when no longer needed

pages.title: Live Events | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API operation for registering LiveEvent objects that record in-match events occurring at specific coordinates during scheduled matches. Serves as the primary mechanism to capture game data (goals, moves, fouls) and map them to Cubes for scoring and rewards, supporting manual, AI-driven, or application-based event submission.

codeExamples.description: Registers a live event (e.g., goal or pass) at coordinates x:45, y:40 during a match, triggering reward processing for cube owners at that location
Successful response confirming the live event was recorded and mapped to corresponding cubes for scoring

useCases: Record goals in real-time soccer matches and reward fans owning cubes in goal zones
Capture chess moves automatically via AI and assign points to cube owners
Manually register fouls or penalties during live sports events via organizer dashboard
Integrate BWS machine learning models for automated game event detection and submission
Build custom applications that push live match data to the FAN Game Cube API

implementationSteps.title: Prepare Event Data
implementationSteps.description: Gather matchId, fieldId, calendarId, event coordinates (x, y), and eventTypeId for the specific in-game occurrence

implementationSteps.title: Submit Live Event
implementationSteps.description: Call new_event operation with event details to record the action and trigger automatic reward calculations for affected cube owners

implementationSteps.title: Choose Integration Method
implementationSteps.description: Implement manual dashboard entry, integrate BWS AI models for automated detection, or build custom application event submission based on your needs

It is the primary mechanism by which game data—such as goals, moves, fouls, or plays—is captured and mapped to corresponding Cubes for scoring and reward processing. This API serves as a trusted interface to power fan engagement: Manually via the organizer dashboard or solution UI (BWS Solution Interface) Automatically via BWS-provided machine learning models that detect and push game events in real time (e.g., AI-driven goal recognition, chess move capture) Through your application. new_event POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_event parameters * JSON check method parameters new_event Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id for which you want to register the event. fieldId string The Field Id the match is linked to. calendarId string The calendar Id the match is linked to. event JSON check event parameter new_event Event Parameter Parameter Type Desciption coordinates JSON check coordinates parameter eventTypeId string The Event Type Id of the event you want to register. new_event Coordinates Parameter Parameter Type Desciption x string The 2D x coordinate of the event. y string The 2D y coordinate of the event. new_event API Call Example new_event API call using Node.js new_event Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Match | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Match objects in the FAN Game Cube system. Covers creating, updating, listing, and deleting time-bound gameplay sessions tied to specific fields and calendars. Includes match status tracking, team information, pricing, and IPFS image storage integration.

codeExamples.description: Creates a new match in the FAN Game Cube system with calendar assignment, start time, teams, and match image
Lists matches for a specific calendar, optionally filtering by matchId to retrieve single match details
Response from list_matches API call showing match details with IPFS URL for uploaded images

useCases: Create football matches like PSG vs Manchester City with team information and images
Track chess tournament rounds like Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura
Schedule basketball games between Green Bay Packers and Chicago Bears
Update match status through lifecycle: scheduled, playing, finished, canceled
Set cube pricing per match in USD cents for fan NFT purchases

implementationSteps.title: Create Match with Calendar
implementationSteps.description: Use new_match operation with required calendarId and startTimeInMillis parameters, along with match details like name, description, team information, and base64 encoded images

implementationSteps.title: Update Match Details
implementationSteps.description: Call update_match with matchId and calendarId to modify specific fields like status, description, or pricing without affecting other properties

implementationSteps.title: Manage Match Lifecycle
implementationSteps.description: Track matches through their lifecycle by updating status values (scheduled, playing, finished, canceled) and generate reports using list_matches to retrieve current state

Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API Calendar Field Cubes Plays (Field-Calendar) Match Prizes Event Types Live Events ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Cube API Match A Match is a time-bound, gameplay session tied to a specific Field, during which events are tracked, points are awarded, and fan interactions occur. It represents a game instance—such as a football match, chess round, or tournament heat—scheduled via the Calendar object and hosted on a defined Field .\ Examples: PSG vs. Manchester City Green Bay Packers vs Chicago Bears Magnus Carlsen vs Hikaru Nakamura Images The images sent using base64 encoding are saved into IPFS using the BWS IPFS Solution. When getting object details, the IPFS URL is returned. new_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string new_match parameters * JSON check method parameters new_match Method Parameters Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and startTimeInMillis). Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match should be included in. startTimeInMillis * number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled". priceInCents number The default price in USD cents for all the cubes if no specific price is defined for a cube (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD). new_match API Call Example new_match API call using Node.js new_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the newly created match. update_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string update_match parameters * JSON check method parameters update_match Method Parameters Optionals All parameters are optional (except calendarId and matchId) Only provide those you want to update. Parameter Type Desciption calendarId * string Calendar Id the match is included in. matchId * string The Match Id we want to update. startTimeInMillis number The expected match start time in milliseconds. name string The match short name. description string Match description. image string An image base64 encoded string to announce the match. team1Name string The team name (e.g. Manchester City) team1Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. team2Name string The team name (e.g. LA Lakers) team2Flag string An image base64 ecoded string representing the team flag. status string The match status. Use one of the following values: "scheduled", "playing", "finished", "canceled" priceInCents number The price in USD cents (e.g. 1000 for 10 USD) update_match API Call Example update_match API call using Node.js update_match Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matchId for the updated match. list_matches POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string list_matches parameters JSON check method parameters list_matches Method Parameters (optional) Parameter Type Desciption calendar_id string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. matchId string (optional) The Match Id we want to list. list_matches API Call Example list_matches API call using Node.js list_matches Call Response When the API call is successfully executed, it returns the matches for the provided calendar. If we give a Match Id, it will just return the match we want to list. delete_match POST https://api.bws.ninja/v1/call Request Body Name Type Description solution * string BWS.NFT.GameCube operation * string delete_calendar parameters * JSON check method parameters delete_match Method Parameters Parameter Type Desciption matchId string The Match Id of the match we want to delete. calendarId string The Calendar Id the match is linked to. delete_match API Call Example delete_match API call using Node.js delete_match Call Response When the API call is executed without errors, it returns a successful status code. pages.title: Plays (Field-Calendar) | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Field-Calendar relationships in the NFT Game Cube solution. Models the temporal lifecycle of sports fields by linking static field configurations with dynamic calendar entries representing individual matches, tournaments, or seasons. Enables IPFS image storage for calendar branding.

codeExamples.description: Creates a field-calendar relationship linking a sports field to a calendar with description and branded image
Lists all calendars linked to a specific field, showing active seasons or tournaments
Response showing created field-calendar relationship with IPFS URL for the uploaded calendar image

useCases: Create PSG Champions League 2026 season calendar on team's home field
Track New York Knicks 2026 NBA Season with dedicated basketball court configuration
Link multiple tournament calendars to same sports field for different events
Organize field usage across seasons with separate calendar entries
Brand each season or tournament with unique images stored on IPFS

implementationSteps.title: Create Field-Calendar Link
implementationSteps.description: Use new_field-calendar operation with fieldId and calendarId to establish the relationship, including description (e.g., 'PSG 2025-26') and optional base64 encoded image

implementationSteps.title: List Field Calendars
implementationSteps.description: Query all calendars associated with a field using list_field-calendars to view active and historical seasons or tournaments

implementationSteps.title: Remove Calendar Association
implementationSteps.description: Delete the field-calendar relationship using delete_field-calendar when a season or tournament ends or needs to be archived

pages.title: Prizes | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: API reference for managing Prize objects in the FAN Game Cube platform. Covers creating and managing rewards for cube owners based on match or season performance. Supports digital NFTs, tokens, physical goods, and experiences with configurable positioning (1st, 2nd, 3rd place) and rich media descriptions.

codeExamples.description: Creates a first place prize for a match with name, description, and snapshot image stored on IPFS
Lists all prizes configured for a specific match including IPFS URLs for images
Updates prize details with HTML formatted description for rich media presentation

useCases: Award signed merchandise to top cube owners in football matches
Distribute VIP match tickets as prizes for season-long performance
Offer Zoom calls or AMAs with athletes as exclusive rewards
Grant bonus $BWS tokens to top three performers in tournaments
Create tiered prize structures with 1st, 2nd, and 3rd place rewards

implementationSteps.title: Create Match Prize
implementationSteps.description: Use new_match-prize with matchId, calendarId, position (1-3), name, description, and snapshot image to define a reward for cube performance

implementationSteps.title: Add Rich Media Content
implementationSteps.description: Enhance prizes with htmlDescription and multiple images (image1, image2, image3) to create compelling reward presentations

implementationSteps.title: Manage Prize Lifecycle
implementationSteps.description: Update prize details as needed using update_match-prize or remove prizes with delete_match-prize when matches conclude

pages.title: FAN Game Overview | BWS API Docs
pages.summary: Overview of the FAN Game Cube platform for building new revenue streams in the sports industry. Explains how fans can own NFT-based digital assets linked to field sections across football, chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, and other sports, earning points and rewards as real-time events unfold.
useCases: Divide football stadiums into 500-2000 cubes for premium area ownership
Tokenize 64 chessboard squares for chess tournament fan engagement
Split tennis courts into service boxes and baseline zones for match participation
Create cricket field cubes covering batting zones and boundary sections
Enable basketball court tokenization including three-point arc and paint zone

implementationSteps.title: Define Cube Allocation Strategy
implementationSteps.description: Determine number of cubes (500-2000 for football) and pricing strategy based on field position value. Prime areas get higher prices, secondary zones at $10 or lower to encourage broad participation.

implementationSteps.title: Integrate One-Click Widget
implementationSteps.description: Embed the one-click purchase widget into club website using provided JavaScript. Fans can buy cubes with credit cards just like purchasing game tickets, with no special accounts required.

implementationSteps.title: Launch Event and Track Engagement
implementationSteps.description: Publish the widget to start sales. Fans manage cubes via mobile app, track upcoming matches in real-time, and compete on live leaderboards for prizes while cubes trade on secondary marketplaces.

pages.fullContent: BWS API Docs Ctrl k Contact Us Create Account Sign In More Welcome! Quick Start Platform Fees API How Tos Certificate Of Trust Media Assets PLATFORM APIs BWS.IPFS.Upload BWS.Blockchain.Save BWS.Blockchain.Hash BWS.NFT.zK Marketplace Solutions Blockchain Badges FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview FAN Game Cube API ESG Credits X Bot Powered by GitBook On this page Copy Marketplace Solutions FAN Game Cube FAN Game Overview Building New Revenue Streams for the Sports Industry BWS's FAN Game Cube platform revolutionizes fan engagement by allowing sports enthusiasts to own digital assets linked to specific sections of a sports field. This extends beyond football to include chess, tennis, cricket, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, and any sport with a field that can be split into "cubes". As real-time events unfold in their selected area, fans earn points and unlock exciting rewards, all while creating new revenue opportunities for sports clubs, former athletes, influencers, and other key figures in the sports industry. Want to launch a FAN Game Cube event? Contact us here . Cube Allocation for Different Sports Sports organizers can define how many cubes to sell and how to divide their playing area based on the sport’s unique characteristics. A football/soccer field cubes division. Examples include: Football stadiums It is typically divided into 500, 1000, or 2000 cubes , ensuring key areas like penalty boxes and midfield zones hold premium value. Chess tournaments The 64 chessboard squares can be tokenized, offering fans a unique way to interact with their favorite chess events. Tennis courts Tokenizing the baseline, service boxes, and net area allows fans to engage in real-time match events. Cricket fields Dividing the pitch into batting zones, fielding areas, and boundary sections enhances fan participation. Basketball courts Cube allocation can include key areas like the three-point arc, free-throw line, and paint zone . Rugby fields Fans can purchase cubes covering try zones, scrums, and midfield sections . Baseball fields Key locations such as bases, outfield zones, and pitcher’s mound can be tokenized. And a long etcetera. NFT Technology for Ownership and Trading FAN Game Cube utilizes NFT technology to ensure verifiable ownership. However, the usage of NFT goes beyond ownership, allowing fans to trade their cubes as they would with traditional assets. During a season or tournament, cube owners can buy and sell cubes mid-season on secondary marketplaces, generating additional royalties for sports clubs and organizers. Since NFTs offer permanent and transparent ownership records , fans gain long-term value while clubs benefit from secondary sales. Pricing and Monetization Strategy Organizers have complete control over pricing, ensuring dynamic valuation based on strategic cube placement. Different pricing strategies include: Prime field areas Higher prices can be charged for high-demand sections, such as the center of a football pitch, penalty spots, or key cricket fielding positions. Secondary zones To encourage broad participation, less strategic locations, such as side areas or backfield positions, can be offered at $10 or lower. Dynamic pricing models Pricing can fluctuate based on match importance, demand, and historical engagement levels. Revenue Potential Your expected revenue is calculated as follows: Number of cubes × Average Price per cube = Total income For example, launching 500 cubes at an average price of $500 per cube on a VIP event can generate $250,000 in revenue. Larger stadiums, premium tournaments, or full-season FAN Game Cubes can scale this model to generate even higher revenues. Seamless Integration with One-Click Purchase Widget BWS provides a one-click purchase widget for frictionless transactions. Sports clubs do not need to create special accounts or navigate complex payment gateways—cubes can be sold in just a few hours. A weekend Chess Tournament NFT Game Cube event. The widget integrates easily into existing club websites, allowing fans to purchase cubes as simply as buying a regular game ticket. How Fans Can Participate and Win Once a sports club publishes its one-click buy widget , fans can easily purchase FAN Game Cubes using standard credit cards, making participation straightforward and accessible. One-click FAN Game Cube purchase experience This experience brings a new layer of engagement to the fan experience while giving them direct ownership of in-game digital assets. Real-Time Fan Engagement and Play & Win Mechanics NFT-based cubes offer fans an engaging way to experience their favorite sports, providing: True ownership of digital sports assets. Fans can buy, sell, or hold tradeable assets for future value (full-season FAN Game Cube). Passive earnings potential through resale royalties and long-term value accumulation. Access to real-time engagement through a dedicated mobile app. Once a cube is purchased, fans can: Manage their cube collection across multiple sports. Track upcoming matches in real time. Join live leaderboards to compete with other fans for top prizes. Why Choose FAN Game Cube? Multi-Sport Scalability Works across various sports, including football, cricket, chess, basketball, rugby, baseball, hockey, golf, Formula 1, and esports. It is flexible and adaptable to different event structures and fan bases. One-Click Buy Widget A JavaScript widget for effortless integration that enhances the club’s purchasing experience with quick, seamless transactions. Easy & Free to Launch Clubs and organizers can set up Fan Game Cube events using a simple three-step workflow or API calls. Flexible Event Calendars Supports single VIP games, full-season matches, and tournaments. It is scalable, from small local events like the Ohio Chess Tournament to major leagues like NBA teams. Real-Time Fan Engagement A comprehensive mobile app enables clubs to foster deep connections with fans during matches. Features include live-streaming for real-time game viewing and interactive chat to enhance fan engagement and discussion. BWS Fan Game Cube is the future of sports engagement, bridging the digital and real-world experiences for fans while unlocking new revenue opportunities for sports organizations. Get started today and transform the way fans interact with their favorite games! Previous FAN Game Cube Next FAN Game Cube API Last updated 15 days ago Was this helpful? useCases: Track crypto project's X engagement and community activity for competitions
Monitor KOL team performance across multiple accounts in one dashboard
Build verifiable portfolio of X metrics for individual KOLs to get hired
Discover crypto investment opportunities with authentic community traction
Generate automated daily reports showing top contributors and engagement trends
